title: Micranthes svetlanae
intro: Micranthes svetlanae, also known as Svetlana's Saxifrage, is a rare and understudied perennial plant species native to Russia and Eastern Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, likely due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Micranthes svetlanae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Micranthes svetlanae is typically used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often combined with other plants to enhance its therapeutic properties.

Within the Saxifragaceae family, Micranthes svetlanae can be further classified as belonging to the genus Micranthes, which is a group of small to medium-sized flowering plants. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other species.
